records.ety: [F. alluvion, L. alluvio, fr. alluere to wash against; ad + luere, equiv. to lavare, to wash. See Lave.]

defs: Wash or flow of water against the shore or bank.
An overflowing; an inundation; a flood.
Matter deposited by an inundation or the action of flowing water; alluvium.
quotes: The golden alluvions are there [in California and Australia] spread over a far wider space: they are found not only on the banks of rivers, and in their beds, but are scattered over the surface of vast plains.

defs: An accession of land gradually washed to the shore or bank by the flowing of water. See Accretion.
